rulings.Case Title: TONY FU VS. DEMAS YAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 14, 2023 line 1. CROSS-DEFENDANTS BRYANT FU AND CRYSTAL LEI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is GRANTED. The undisputed material facts as to the dates of the transfers alleged in the operative Cross-Complaints (the latest in 2003) establish that as a matter of law at the time of the filing of the Cross-Complaint in 2017 the right of action was extinguished by the statute of repose as a cause of action that has not been filed "within seven years after the transfer was made" no matter whether the claim is "brought under the UFTA or otherwise" is barred. PGA West Residential Assn., Inc. v. Hulven Internat., Inc., (2017) 14 Cal.App.5th 156. The Cross-Complaint, as currently alleged, does not include any factual allegations as to any transfers that occurred within 7 years of the filing of the cross-complaint. Request for continuance is denied. There is no indication that discovery will be relevant to the issue of the statute of repose. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CYRUS ESTEBAN ET AL VS. CIVIC FINANCIAL SERVICES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 14, 2023 line 2. DEFENDANT CIVIC FINANCIAL SERVICES, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. The allegations in the complaint combined with judicially noticeable documents are: (1) Plaintiffs sought to refinance their existing loan in February 2019 (Complaint, paragraph 9); (2) Plaintiffs were approached by defendant on March 25, 2019 regarding a re-finance offer (Complaint, paragraph 11); (3) Plaintiffs executed the Deed of Trust securing a Note on March 28, 2019 and the Deed of Trust was recorded on March 29, 2019 (Deed of Trust, Assignment of Leases and Rents, Security Agreement, and Fixture Filing); (3) at some point between March 25, 2019 (when Plaintiff where approached by Defendants) and March 28, 2019 (when Plaintiff obtained the operative loan), Plaintiffs "passed on opportunities with other lenders" (Complaint, paragraph 11, 12); (5) on April 1, 2019 Plaintiffs were informed of the favorable terms of the loan (Complaint, paragraph 11); (5) on an unspecified date in April 2019 Plaintiffs were informed that the terms of the offered loan have changed to less favorable (Complaint, paragraph 14); (6) Plaintiffs told Eddie Salem that these loan terms were not what he proposed before, but he was unwilling to do anything to change the final loan terms. Plaintiffs had to take the loan because their existing loan was coming due and they were out of time at that point. (Complaint, paragraph 16). In opposition to the demurrer (no courtesy copy received in violation of SFRL 2.7B) Plaintiff fails to address the inconsistent timeline: recorded document dated March 28, 2019 vs. the purported date of the execution of the documents in "April 2019." Plaintiffs fails to allege any facts or explain in opposition how Plaintiffs were damaged by or relied on the representation made in April 2019 regarding their loan obtained in March 2019. Plaintiffs were provided multiple opportunities to amend their complaint. They did not do so. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
